description: Initial baseline run for system-wide integration performance test case. Failed immediately.

asi.error_details: Exit code 2 observed. This indicates a failure in the core integration workload. Need to review code paths in ar_system, ar_agency, ar_delegation, etc., to find the root cause of the failure before retrying performance measurements.
asi.initial_debug_step: Review build logs and test output for the root cause of failure 2.

description: Fixed critical build linking issue on macOS by updating Makefile to correctly link libC for Zig tests. Verified with 'make build' and 'make check-logs'.

asi.fix_applied: Updated Makefile to set ZIG_TEST_LINK_FLAGS and ZIG_TEST_TSAN_FLAGS to correctly link libc on macOS (Darwin) and appropriately adjust TSan flags.
asi.issue_resolved: macOS linking failure for Zig tests due to missing system C library linkage definitions.

description: Crash: benchmark harness failed at link time because autoresearch.sh links against nonexistent -lar_system instead of the built libagerun archive; no performance measurement collected.

asi.hypothesis: Current autoresearch.sh benchmark harness can be built and run as-is to establish a baseline total_µs measurement.
asi.rollback_reason: Run crashed before producing any metric because the benchmark command failed to link on macOS.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch experiment command to the faster make ar_integration_performance_tests target requested by the user and ensure that target emits a stable total_µs metric for autoresearch.
asi.error_details: ld: library 'ar_system' not found; clang linker exited 1 during ./autoresearch.sh
asi.learning: The current benchmark wrapper is broken independently of runtime performance, so the experiment command itself must be corrected before further optimization.

description: Crash: attempted to switch the benchmark command directly to make ar_integration_performance_tests, but autoresearch runner enforces using autoresearch.sh when that file exists.

asi.hypothesis: The experiment loop can directly run make ar_integration_performance_tests after reinitializing the session for the new target.
asi.rollback_reason: The autoresearch harness rejected the custom command because autoresearch.sh is present and mandatory in this repo.
asi.next_action_hint: Update autoresearch.sh itself to invoke make ar_integration_performance_tests so the loop uses the requested faster-turnaround target.
asi.error_details: run_experiment rejected custom command and instructed to use ./autoresearch.sh instead
asi.learning: In this harness, changing the experiment command requires changing autoresearch.sh, not only autoresearch.md or init_experiment metadata.

description: Crash: new make-based benchmark target builds and starts, but agent creation fails at agent 64 before any metric is emitted.

asi.hypothesis: A dedicated make ar_integration_performance_tests benchmark target with a ring-node method can run the 100-agent/1000-message workload and emit total_µs quickly enough for iterative autoresearch.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark cannot establish a baseline because ar_agency__create_agent starts failing partway through ring construction.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect agent-capacity limits or initialization failures around the 64th agent, then either align the benchmark with actual supported capacity or remove the bottleneck in agent creation.
asi.error_details: make ar_integration_performance_tests failed with 'Failed to create benchmark agent 64' after repeated methodology lookup logs
asi.learning: The requested faster-turnaround harness is wired up correctly enough to compile and execute, but the runtime currently cannot sustain the 100-agent benchmark workload.

description: Crash: autoresearch.sh now runs make ar_integration_performance_tests as requested, but the benchmark still fails during setup when creating benchmark agent 64.

asi.hypothesis: Switching autoresearch.sh to make ar_integration_performance_tests will provide the faster benchmark loop requested by the user and allow a baseline run.
asi.rollback_reason: The command switch worked, but the benchmark itself still crashes before emitting total_µs because agent creation fails at 64.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate the 64-agent creation ceiling in runtime structures or adjust the benchmark only if the documented workload is provably unsupported and the objective is updated.
asi.error_details: ./autoresearch.sh executes make ar_integration_performance_tests; make fails with 'Failed to create benchmark agent 64'
asi.learning: The experiment command is now correctly switched to the make target; remaining failures are in runtime capacity/benchmark setup, not in the harness command.

description: Crash: increasing ar_map capacity removed the 64-agent registry ceiling, but the new benchmark still failed after one message because agents were created with NULL context and frame creation requires non-NULL context.

asi.hypothesis: Raising fixed ar_map capacity above 100 and using the make-based benchmark harness will be enough to establish a successful 100-agent baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: The runtime progressed past agent creation but failed in execution because ar_frame__create requires non-NULL context for each agent.
asi.next_action_hint: Rebuild the benchmark to keep a shared context map alive for all agents during the entire run, then retry the baseline.
asi.error_details: Benchmark stopped after 1 message with 'ERROR: Failed to create execution frame' and log evidence showed agent 1 executed before failing.
asi.learning: The 64-agent failure was caused by the fixed-size ar_map in agent registry tracking, but the benchmark also must satisfy the non-NULL context prerequisite documented for frame creation.

description: Established first successful make-based integration benchmark baseline by raising fixed ar_map capacity from 64 to 128 for 100-agent registry tracking and keeping a shared context alive for all benchmark agents.

asi.hypothesis: The benchmark can be made to run successfully by removing the 64-entry registry bottleneck in ar_map and satisfying the frame subsystem's non-NULL context requirement with a shared benchmark context.
asi.root_cause: ar_agent_registry uses ar_map internally; fixed MAP_SIZE 64 prevented tracking the 65th+ agent, and NULL agent contexts caused ar_frame__create to fail during execution.
asi.benchmark_setup: autoresearch.sh now runs make ar_integration_performance_tests; benchmark creates 100 ring agents with shared context and processes 1000 messages.
asi.learning: The benchmark currently spends substantial time printing per-message output and debug logs, which is likely the dominant remaining performance bottleneck.
asi.context_fix: modules/ar_integration_performance_tests.c now passes one shared map context to all agents for the duration of the run

description: Discarded removal of send/system/interpreter hot-path logging because the measured total_µs regressed from 88,115 to 96,394 despite substantially reducing output volume.

asi.hypothesis: The dominant cost in the successful baseline is per-message stdout/stderr logging in ar_system, ar_agency, ar_agent, ar_interpreter, and send evaluation, so removing those hot-path prints should improve total_µs.
asi.rollback_reason: Measured primary metric regressed versus the committed baseline, so this change is not a clear improvement yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Target the remaining hot-path DEBUG emitters visible in benchmark output (method evaluator, frame creation, memory access) or re-run a narrower logging-removal variant to separate noise from real effect.
asi.learning: Output volume dropped a lot, but the benchmark still emits many DEBUG lines from deeper evaluator/frame paths, and this broader logging-removal patch did not produce an immediate win on total_µs.
asi.observed_remaining_logs: DEBUG [METHOD_EVAL], DEBUG [MEMORY_ACCESS], DEBUG [FRAME_CREATE], plus agency lookup info lines remain in output.

description: Improved baseline by removing deeper per-message debug logging from frame creation, method evaluation, and message memory access while keeping the successful 100-agent benchmark harness intact.

asi.hypothesis: The remaining dominant benchmark overhead after baseline establishment is deeper evaluator/frame debug logging on every processed message, so removing those logs should reduce total_µs even if higher-level system prints remain.
asi.learning: Removing deep evaluator/frame debug output produced a clear win to 80,071µs, so hot-path logging is a real bottleneck. The remaining obvious timed output is the per-message print in ar_system__process_next_message.
asi.next_promising_step: Try removing the remaining per-message output in ar_system__process_next_message and re-measure; if still worthwhile, inspect any residual logging on send paths.

description: Slightly improved total_µs by removing the remaining per-message stdout print from ar_system__process_next_message; benchmark output is now dominated by send/agent/interpreter debug logs.

asi.hypothesis: The remaining stdout print in ar_system__process_next_message still adds measurable overhead to the timed loop even after deeper evaluator/frame debug logging was removed.
asi.learning: The system-level per-message print was still on the hot path and trimming it produced a small additional gain. Remaining benchmark output now comes mostly from DEBUG [SEND], DEBUG [AGENT_SEND], and DEBUG [INTERPRETER].
asi.next_promising_step: Remove send/agent/interpreter hot-path debug logging next and re-measure against the new 79,797µs baseline.

description: Improved total_µs by removing remaining send/agent/interpreter hot-path debug logging; benchmark output dropped to setup-time methodology lookup logs only.

asi.hypothesis: The remaining per-message debug logging in ar_agency__send_to_agent, ar_agent__send, and ar_interpreter__execute_method is still a material throughput bottleneck after removing deeper evaluator/frame logs and system stdout.
asi.learning: This was a strong win, confirming that hot-path logging dominated runtime overhead. The remaining visible logs are methodology lookup info lines during benchmark setup, which are outside the timed loop.
asi.next_promising_step: Inspect structural overhead in message dispatch and agent scanning inside ar_system__process_next_message; removing O(agent_count) scanning per message may yield the next meaningful improvement.

description: Massively improved total_µs by adding a rolling next-agent search hint in ar_system__process_next_message, eliminating the repeated scan from the first agent on every message dispatch.

asi.hypothesis: After removing hot-path logging, the next dominant cost is the O(agent_count) scan from the first agent on every ar_system__process_next_message call; caching a rolling start point should reduce queue discovery to near-O(1) for workloads like the ring benchmark.
asi.algorithm_change: Added next_agent_hint to ar_system state; message scanning now starts from the previous hit, wraps once through the registry, and resets when the agent set is empty or the hint becomes invalid.
asi.learning: Queue-discovery scanning was the overwhelming structural bottleneck. Starting each search near the last successful agent reduced total_µs from 75,569 to 15,750.
asi.next_promising_step: Inspect setup-time methodology lookup logging and agent-creation overhead only if future benchmarks include setup in the timed region; for the current timed loop, deeper structural costs are now much smaller.

description: Further improved queue dispatch by advancing the next-agent hint to the current agent only when it still has queued messages, otherwise to the next registry entry, avoiding one wasted check per handoff in the ring workload.

asi.hypothesis: The rolling search hint can be refined further by pointing directly at the most likely next message owner after execution instead of always restarting from the just-processed agent.
asi.algorithm_refinement: After execution, next_agent_hint now stays on the current agent only if it still has messages; otherwise it advances to the next registered agent and wraps to the first agent if needed.
asi.learning: Once the major scan bottleneck was removed, there was still a smaller but measurable cost from checking the already-emptied agent first on each handoff.
asi.next_promising_step: Look at lower-level ownership/copy overhead in send(message) and expression evaluation, especially claim_or_copy and repeated agency/method lookups inside the timed loop.

description: Improved total_µs by removing the redundant agent_has_messages pre-check and probing queues directly with ar_agency__get_agent_message during dispatch scanning.

asi.hypothesis: After optimizing the scan start point, the dispatch loop still wastes work by looking up each candidate agent twice—once for has_messages and again for get_agent_message—so collapsing to a single queue probe should help.
asi.algorithm_refinement: ar_system__process_next_message now attempts ar_agency__get_agent_message directly while scanning instead of calling ar_agency__agent_has_messages first.
asi.learning: Even after the large cursor optimization, duplicate registry/queue lookups in the hot path were still measurable.
asi.next_promising_step: Investigate whether ar_agency__get_agent_method/get_agent_mutable_memory/get_agent_context repeated registry lookups inside ar_interpreter__execute_method can be collapsed or cached.

description: Collapsed three registry lookups per executed message into one by having ar_interpreter__execute_method fetch the agent once from the registry and then read method/memory/context directly from the agent.

asi.hypothesis: With dispatch scanning mostly fixed, repeated registry lookups inside ar_interpreter__execute_method are now a major hot-path cost; using one agent lookup instead of three agency wrapper lookups should improve total_µs.
asi.algorithm_change: Interpreter now gets the registry once, finds the target agent once, and calls ar_agent accessors directly for method, mutable memory, and context.
asi.learning: Registry lookup overhead remained substantial even after the dispatch cursor optimization. Eliminating two extra lookups per message cut total_µs from 14,959 to 10,157.
asi.next_promising_step: Inspect send-path lookups similarly: ar_agency__send_to_agent and ar_system dispatch still route through registry-based agent discovery that may be reducible or cacheable.

description: Greatly improved registry hot-path performance by making ar_agent_registry find/untrack use direct formatted ID keys instead of scanning the registered-ID list to recover the key string before every map lookup.

asi.hypothesis: Agent registry lookup still does avoidable O(agent_count) work because find_agent first scans the registered ID list to reconstruct a key string; formatting the numeric ID directly should cut a large amount of hot-path overhead.
asi.algorithm_change: ar_agent_registry__find_agent and ar_agent_registry__untrack_agent now build the numeric string key directly with snprintf and query/remove from ar_map without a preliminary registered-list scan.
asi.learning: Registry lookup design was still a major bottleneck. Eliminating the list scan before each map lookup dropped total_µs from 10,157 to 7,587.
asi.next_promising_step: Examine whether ar_map itself can avoid linear scans for these numeric-string keys or whether benchmark hot paths can bypass string-keyed maps altogether.

description: Baseline re-run to measure noise on the current best registry-lookup optimization; total_µs landed slightly above the 7,587µs best, indicating low-thousands-of-ns jitter rather than a new regression.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the current best commit will estimate benchmark noise before making the next structural optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; this run is a confirmation sample and did not beat the current best metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue the next structural hot-path optimization, most likely reducing linear string-key scans in ar_map or avoiding repeated methodology lookup/logging during benchmark setup.
asi.learning: Current best appears stable around 7.6ms, so improvements now need to clear a relatively small noise floor.

description: Improved total_µs by replacing ar_map's full-table linear string scan with hashed open addressing plus tombstones, cutting registry/map hot-path lookups across dispatch, send, and memory access.

asi.hypothesis: The next dominant overhead after direct agent-ID key formatting is ar_map itself, which still linearly scans all 128 slots on every lookup/update; switching to hashed open addressing should reduce registry and map access cost.
asi.algorithm_change: Added FNV-1a hashing, linear-probed open addressing, and tombstone-aware deletion while preserving the existing ar_map API and ownership semantics.
asi.learning: Generic map lookup remained a major bottleneck even after registry-level improvements. Moving from O(MAP_SIZE) string scans to hash-guided probing reduced total_µs from 7,587 to 6,257.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect remaining hot-path work in message dispatch and send/evaluation, especially repeated agent existence/next-agent queries and frame/message ownership operations.

description: Improved total_µs by making agent-registry iteration and membership O(1) with indexed next/prev/registered arrays, removing the per-message list copy/scan work in get_first/get_next/is_registered.

asi.hypothesis: After hashing ar_map, the next hot path is agent-registry traversal itself: get_first/get_next/is_registered still allocate list snapshots and scan/parse ID strings, so replacing those lookups with indexed linkage should reduce dispatch overhead further.
asi.supporting_checks: make ar_agent_registry_tests 2>&1 and make ar_map_tests 2>&1 both passed before benchmarking
asi.algorithm_change: Agent registry now maintains first/last IDs plus indexed next/prev/registered/key arrays alongside the existing ID-string list, making iteration and membership checks O(1) while preserving public behavior and stable key ownership.
asi.learning: Even after fixing map lookup complexity, registry traversal remained a major bottleneck. Eliminating list snapshot allocation and repeated string-to-int parsing cut total_µs from 6,257 to 4,604.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate expression-evaluator memory access overhead next, especially per-message path-array/full-path allocation for memory.next_id and message access.

description: Improved total_µs by removing per-message argument-list snapshot allocation in send evaluation, using ar_list__first/ar_list__last instead of ar_list__items for the fixed two-argument send AST.

asi.hypothesis: Send evaluation still allocates an array for its two fixed argument ASTs on every message; replacing that with direct first/last list access should trim allocator and copy overhead in the hot path.
asi.algorithm_change: ar_send_instruction_evaluator__evaluate now reads the two send arguments directly from the list without allocating a temporary items array.
asi.learning: Small per-message allocations are still materially expensive at this scale. Removing just one temporary array allocation per message reduced total_µs from 4,604 to 3,765.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for similar fixed-arity or repeated list/path snapshot allocations in expression and instruction evaluation, especially memory-access evaluation.

description: Improved total_µs by avoiding memory-path snapshot and full-path allocation for the common single-segment access case, using new borrowed AST path accessors so memory.next_id/message access stay allocation-free.

asi.hypothesis: Expression evaluation still allocates path arrays and concatenated path strings for each memory access; the benchmark's hot path only needs zero- or one-segment accesses, so fast-pathing those should reduce allocator overhead further.
asi.supporting_checks: make ar_expression_ast_tests 2>&1, make ar_expression_evaluator_tests 2>&1, and make ar_send_instruction_evaluator_tests 2>&1 all passed before benchmarking
asi.algorithm_change: Added borrowed memory-path count/component AST accessors and taught the expression evaluator to bypass path-array/full-string allocation for zero- and one-segment accesses.
asi.learning: Memory-access path materialization was still expensive in the ring benchmark. Fast-pathing simple access patterns reduced total_µs from 3,765 to 3,125.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe remaining hot-path allocations and ownership churn next, especially message/frame ownership transitions and any residual list snapshot creation in evaluator/executor code.
